page: Amazon Web Services c9gd.xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. This instance features an arm64 CPU architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated resource allocation with a single thread per physical core.

For local storage requirements, the c9gd.xlarge includes a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. This local storage configuration supports applications requiring high-speed, low-latency temporary storage. The networking architecture provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

The ARM-based architecture of the AWS Graviton processor combined with the Nitro hypervisor delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance suitable for compute-intensive tasks that also require fast local scratch space. Appropriate workloads include distributed analytics, batch processing, ad serving, video encoding, and containerized microservices that benefit from local NVMe storage.
